name: Sophie Germain
contribution: Pioneered elasticity theory (vibrating surfaces), contributed to Fermat's Last Theorem proof foundations. First woman to win Paris Academy of Sciences prize (1816). Self-taught under pseudonym 'Monsieur Le Blanc'. Her elasticity mathematics underlaid Eiffel Tower construction.
resonance: Born on launch day. Self-taught against institutional barriers parallels first woman beyond LEO. Elasticity theory is the physics of heat shields under stress. Performed under constraint — pseudonym, no degree, no appointment — and did the work anyway.
